attacking_style: Typically attacks from established breakaways on final climbs (35-45km to go), then exploits technical descents to build gaps. Prefers tactical marking without taking pulls, preserving sprint legs for final 500m finishes.
team_tactics: Primarily deployed as high-tempo domestique on steep gradients (12%+) for Pogačar. When given freedom, UAE positions him in early breaks or uses him to apply pressure with GC leader protected behind. Mixed results with co-leadership roles.
terrain_preference: Excels on climbing time trials and technical descents with diesel TT power. Strong on steep finishes where sustained tempo matters more than explosive watts. Vulnerable in crosswinds and positioning battles—dropped in echelons losing 2+ minutes.
vulnerability: Consistently dropped on moderate climbs when fatigued from overextension or early-race workload. Critical weakness in crosswind positioning and pack placement. Crash/mechanical incidents suggest bike handling under pressure needs improvement.
key_patterns: Strong time trial progression: multiple top-8 finishes, 4.5sec up at intermediates, early reference times
Dropped on moderate/hilly stages when overextended in domestique duties or defensive workload (3+ instances)
Excels closing large gaps on descents: bridged 40s deficit twice, soloed 45km technical terrain
Tactical maturity in breakaways: marks attacks without working, preserves sprint for technical finishes
Crosswind positioning weakness: lost 2:05 in echelons, abandoned from crashes, mechanicals at critical moments
form_trajectory: positive: First Grand Tour stage win at 25 shows maturation. Top-3 climbing TT splits in 2023 Giro indicate unused GC potential, though early-stage weakness suggests deployment timing issues remain.
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 19
